title: WebSocket streaming subscriptions vector has no upper bound
description: streaming.rs handle_ws: the subscriptions Vec<String> grows unbounded as clients send subscribe commands. A malicious authenticated client can send millions of subscribe commands with unique list IDs (e.g. list:12345:0, list:12345:1, ...) since the list parameter is user-controlled. Each subscription is checked on every broadcast event via .iter().any(), so this degrades broadcast performance for all connected clients. Fix: cap subscriptions to a reasonable limit (e.g. 100) and reject further subscribes, or use a HashSet for O(1) membership checks.
